normalized_title: City of Melville Local Planning Policy 1.4 - Provision of Public Art in Development Proposals
scope_summary: Local planning policy requiring the provision of public art (or cash-in-lieu into the City of Melville Capital Arts Trust) for development applications for multiple dwellings, mixed use or non-residential developments where the nominated development cost exceeds $2 million, anywhere LPS6 applies. It prescribes a sliding-scale contribution based on construction cost, performance standards for the artwork, Public Art Plan information requirements, and installation timing (on-site art before initial occupation; cash-in-lieu before construction commences).

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: multiple dwelling, mixed use and non-residential development applications

key_numeric_standards.value: $500,000 plus 0.5% of every dollar over $50M (additional component paid into public art fund)

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: developments under $15M electing cash-in-lieu instead of on-site art
City of Melville Capital Arts Trust funds
public art consultant and project management fees

definitions: City of Melville Capital Arts Trust

cross_references.cited: Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015
City of Melville Public Art Strategy and Masterplan

quality_flags: extraction quality good; contribution table (Table 1) flattened but values fully recoverable
header states 'Policy Type: Council Policy' while the citation clause states it is a Local Planning Policy prepared under Schedule 2 of the Regulations - source inconsistency
version evidenced by 'Last Review Date: 20 May 2025' header and Ordinary Meeting of Council 20/05/2025 (UP25/67); printed version stamp 6/06/2025
does not apply to single houses or grouped dwellings; applies to multiple dwellings/mixed use over $2M
